Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Serve Verfications To Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCE BY EITHER PARTY. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Morton International, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Serve Verifications To Discovery Requests is granted in part and denied in part. The court does not find that the statements that 'Plaintiffs' investigation and discovery are continuing,' are substantive responses; and no verification is required for that portion of any discovery response.
As to the Requests For Admissions Numbers 1-8, the motion is granted as each response either denies or states that Plaintiffs cannot admit or deny the request. A verification is required for each of those responses.
As to the Form Interrogatories, the motion is granted as to Number 17.1 as it makes a substantive response. A verification is required.
As to the Special Interrogatories, the motion is denied. As to this discovery, no separate statement identifies which interrogatory has a substantive response.
As to the Requests For Production Of Documents, the motion is denied.
Verifications shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
